styles.positive_style_prompt: source-preserving documentary image, one clear subject or scene, subject remains recognizable after simplification, one dominant mechanism-bearing tension or contradiction, controlled palette, restrained lighting, clear silhouette, low clutter, non-spectacular tension, vertical 9:16 frame
styles.negative_prompt: text, watermark, logo, ui overlay, readable interface text, warning labels, document text, code text, poster design, collage fragments, gore, injury close-up, patient suffering, named victim, sensational burn imagery, crowded scene, many small objects, unreadable background detail, motion blur, action pose, blockbuster cinematic lighting, multiple surreal events, competing bright accents

cases.prompt_body: A 1980s radiation therapy treatment room seen as one source-anchored system: a large Therac-like treatment unit and treatment table inside a shielded room, with a separate operator control position implied beyond thick observation glass through a small monitor and intercom. Preserve the machine-room separation from the Therac facility cross-section. No patient is visible. One quiet wrong state: the room looks procedurally normal while remote control feels fragile and isolated. restrained neutrals, clinical low light, documentary realism, no readable labels or interface text.

An anonymous 1980s clinical operator at a remote radiation-therapy control console outside a shielded treatment room, with a small dark monitor view and fixed intercom suggesting the machine is in another room. Preserve the operator and machine separation described in the Therac treatment-session source. One wrong-state cue: non-readable indicator lights and a blank signal conflict with the operator's calm posture. role-based human scene, no identifiable face, no legible screen text, restrained clinical palette, locked documentary frame.

A close source-preserving mechanism view of a radiation-therapy turntable assembly with distinct beam-shaping positions, a target position, a filter or flattener position, and an electron scan path implied by clean physical geometry rather than labels. Preserve the Therac turntable logic as a recognizable rotating assembly, not a generic hospital prop. One contradiction: a high-energy beam path is visually open where an independent physical safeguard should be. muted industrial metal, restrained clinical lighting, no readable diagrams, no labels, no code.

A source-aware machine-family continuity image: one current 1980s radiation-therapy treatment unit in front, with two older treatment-machine silhouettes or hardware outlines receding behind it as inherited architecture, not literal named models. The safety burden reads as one shared control path carried forward from older machines into the newer unit. No green code, no computer text, no documents. The mechanism is borrowed credibility, shown through similar machine forms and a single overburdened control connection. clinical, official, restrained, documentary.

A restrained 1980s clinical control-room response scene with two anonymous staff figures and one manufacturer or supervisor role implied by posture across a desk or phone line, all subordinate to the remote machine console and monitor. Preserve the Therac report-disbelief source logic: information is present but fails to move through the system. One repeated-error cue appears as a non-readable stack of identical blank forms or small unlabelled status slips, never legible text. official normalcy, low clutter, no readable documents, no logos, no patient depiction.

A concrete system-safety mechanism composite anchored to a Therac-like treatment unit and turntable path: one control cable or software-mediated channel appears to carry both command and safety responsibility, while the expected independent physical interlock is absent as an empty bracket or disconnected mechanical position. Preserve machine-room and turntable logic; reject code-only framing. One dominant absence: no separate safety layer between the wrong state and the beam path. muted industrial neutrals, clinical documentary lighting, no warning signs, no labels, no readable UI.

A final consequence carrier for the Therac-25 short: the treatment unit, treatment table, remote observation barrier, and operator console relationship are all readable as one quiet machine system. Preserve the shielded-room geography from the facility cross-section and avoid any title-card feeling. One small cause is implied by a tiny blank control indicator or absent interlock detail, while the room remains calm and clinical. no patient, no gore, no readable text, restrained low-chroma palette, still documentary frame.
